title: SpaceX IPO Targets $2T+ Valuation — Bloomberg Analysis Examines Widening Competitive Moat Ahead of Record Listing
detail: Bloomberg published a detailed analysis on April 24, 2026 — 'SpaceX Is Widening Its Competitive Moat Ahead of Record IPO' — examining how SpaceX's strategic position has strengthened across multiple dimensions in the weeks since its confidential S-1 filing on April 1, 2026. The analysis follows Bloomberg's April 2 report that SpaceX is targeting a valuation above $2 trillion for its planned June 2026 Nasdaq IPO, up from the $1.75T initially cited at the time of filing. Multiple converging moat-widening moves are highlighted: (1) Starlink's scale advantage — 10M+ subscribers in 160 countries with 60%+ gross margins and projected 2026 revenue of $15.9B–$24B (Quilty Space / Bloomberg analysts); (2) the Golden Dome consortium announcement (April 22) adding defense software as a new revenue vertical; (3) a $178.5M U.S. Space Force / Space Development Agency task order for missile-tracking satellite launches; and (4) the $60B Cursor AI acquisition option (April 23) deepening AI integration across SpaceX's engineering stack. At the $2T+ target, SpaceX would surpass Meta as approximately the 5th most valuable public company globally and would raise approximately $75B in a single IPO — dwarfing Saudi Aramco's record $29.4B raise in 2019. The Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs-led offering, with 21 total underwriting banks, targets a 30% retail allocation — three times the industry standard. The public S-1 is expected in late April or May 2026. Amazon Kuiper is noted as the only credible competitor to Starlink, but SpaceX's 24-month operational head start and first-mover advantage in direct-to-cell connectivity (DTC) are seen as substantial barriers to displacement.

media.caption: SpaceX IPO targets $2T+ valuation — analysis of competitive moat ahead of record June 2026 Nasdaq listing
